pattern: The answer is the minimum over all start-to-finish paths of the path's largest elevation, found by a Dijkstra-style expansion that always advances through the lowest reachable cell.
hints: You want the path whose highest cell is as small as possible, since the wait time equals that maximum elevation.
Grow a region from the top-left, always stepping next into the lowest-elevation frontier cell with a min-heap.
Pop the smallest frontier cell, track the running maximum elevation popped, and when you pop the bottom-right cell that running maximum is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: two by two where the corner gates the time
snake pattern forces a high bottleneck
two by two sorted in reading order
the highest elevation sits on every path
three by three increasing in reading order

stress.description: random unique elevation grids covering exactly 0..n*n-1
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(20, max(1, size)))
    vals = list(range(n * n))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    grid = [vals[i * n:(i + 1) * n] for i in range(n)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: values are a permutation of 0..n*n-1 so all elevations are unique
